title: Malty Creamy Canadian Yam Pudding
description: A comforting, malty, and creamy dessert featuring white yam, inspired by traditional Canadian cuisine.

ingredients.name: Non-dairy milk
ingredients.notes: such as almond or soy milk

instructions: Peel and chop the white yams into 1-inch cubes.
In a blender or food processor, blend the yam cubes with the non-dairy milk until smooth.
In a medium saucepan, combine the blended yam mixture, maple syrup, coconut cream, vanilla extract, salt, and malt extract.
Whisk the mixture over medium heat until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
In a small bowl, whisk together the arrowroot powder and 2 tablespoons of water until smooth.
Add the arrowroot mixture to the saucepan and whisk until the mixture thickens.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the coconut oil until melted.
Pour the mixture into individual serving cups or ramekins.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pudding is set and slightly puffed.
Serve warm or chilled, garnished with fresh berries or whipped cream if desired.
